purpose: Human-curated overlay merged by tools/build_strategy_param_specs.py. The OPTIMIZABLE grids are the single source of truth in CODE (mega_walk_forward.GRIDS) and are introspected mechanically; this file only ADDS documentation the code cannot express: why the grid ranges were chosen, which knobs are HARDCODED (fixed, not swept) and why, and candidate MISSING knobs that would be new strategy logic (Faz 3, approval-gated). Editing this file NEVER changes engine behavior — it is documentation only.

phase_legend.1-mechanical: promote an existing hardcoded value to a swept param — same logic, more dial settings (optimization; low risk)
phase_legend.3-RnD: add a NEW decision rule — changes trading behavior = new strategy variant (approval-gated, VARIANT_LOG, fresh validation)

GEN_DONCHIAN_BREAKOUT.grid_rationale: channel_len 10-150 brackets the Turtle-classic 20-55 breakout window plus faster/slower variants; atr_buf filters marginal breakouts by requiring price to clear the channel by a fraction of ATR; stop_lookback sets the swing-low stop window.

fixed_knobs.name: atr_period
fixed_knobs.reason: buffer uses df.atr_14; the ATR window itself is never swept

fixed_knobs.name: exit_style
fixed_knobs.reason: the classic Donchian opposite-channel (N-low) exit is NOT implemented — known gap; exit is a rolling-low stop only

fixed_knobs.name: entry_trigger
fixed_knobs.reason: close-based confirmation; an intrabar high>channel trigger is not offered

missing_knobs.name: opposite_channel_exit
missing_knobs.note: Turtle-signature exit on an N-low breakout instead of a fixed stop

missing_knobs.name: short_side
missing_knobs.note: symmetric short entry on an N-low breakdown

missing_knobs.name: atr_period
missing_knobs.note: promote the fixed ATR=14 to a swept knob

GEN_DONCHIAN_TURTLE.grid_rationale: Faz-3 variant that fills DONCHIAN's missing Turtle STRUCTURAL stop: entry is the same channel breakout, but the stop is placed at the opposite exit_channel_len-low channel (exit_channel_len <= entry_channel_len) instead of the base strategy's short stop_lookback rolling low.

fixed_knobs.name: exit_style
fixed_knobs.reason: TRUE trailing opposite-channel exit needs an engine-core change to simulate_slice (Faz 3b, approval-gated); this variant only relocates the STATIC stop to the opposite channel within the (signal, stop) contract

fixed_knobs.name: profit_target
fixed_knobs.reason: still uses the global 2R target + 96-bar holding limit

missing_knobs.name: trailing_opposite_channel_exit
missing_knobs.note: re-evaluate the opposite-channel exit every bar (full Turtle signature) — requires simulate_slice support

missing_knobs.name: short_side
missing_knobs.note: symmetric short on N-low breakdown — simulate_slice supports is_short but the runner must pass direction

GEN_GOLDEN_CROSS_PULLBACK.grid_rationale: fast/slow EMA pair defines the trend regime (20/50 over 100/150/200); pull_atr sets how close to the fast EMA a pullback entry must be; stop_lookback is the swing-low stop window.

fixed_knobs.name: exit_style
fixed_knobs.reason: no opposite-cross exit; rolling-low stop only

missing_knobs.name: death_cross_exit
missing_knobs.note: exit when fast crosses back below slow

GEN_RSI_OVERSOLD_REVERSAL.grid_rationale: rsi_len sets sensitivity; oversold is the dip threshold; recovery is the level RSI must reclaim to trigger (recovery>oversold enforced).

fixed_knobs.name: stop_window
fixed_knobs.reason: stop = low.rolling(5).min() is HARDCODED, not swept

fixed_knobs.name: trend_filter
fixed_knobs.reason: no higher-timeframe or EMA trend gate — pure oscillator reversal

missing_knobs.name: stop_window
missing_knobs.note: promote fixed 5 to a swept knob

missing_knobs.name: trend_filter
missing_knobs.note: add an ema_200 regime gate as an on/off knob

GEN_MACD_BULL_CROSS.grid_rationale: fast/slow/signal are the three standard MACD periods (fast<slow enforced); the classic 12/26/9 is inside the grid.

fixed_knobs.name: trend_filter
fixed_knobs.reason: ema_200 regime gate is hardcoded on

missing_knobs.name: bear_cross_exit
missing_knobs.note: exit on the opposite MACD cross

GEN_ZSCORE_MEAN_REVERSION.grid_rationale: win is the mean/std lookback; z_lo is the dip entry z-score (buy the dip); trend_ema keeps entries in an uptrend (close>trend_ema).

fixed_knobs.name: exit_style
fixed_knobs.reason: no mean-reversion exit-at-mean; uses the global 2R target

missing_knobs.name: exit_at_mean
missing_knobs.note: close when z reverts to 0 instead of a 2R target — the natural mean-reversion exit

GEN_ATR_PULLBACK_TREND.grid_rationale: ema_len is the trend anchor; dist_atr caps how far above the EMA an entry may be; slope_atr requires a minimum ATR-normalized slope; stop_lookback is the swing-low stop.

fixed_knobs.name: slope_window
fixed_knobs.reason: slope measured over shift(5) — HARDCODED, not swept

missing_knobs.name: slope_window
missing_knobs.note: promote fixed 5-bar slope window to a swept knob

GEN_KELTNER_BREAKOUT.grid_rationale: ema_len is the channel mid; atr_len is the band ATR window; mult sets band width (1.5-3.0).

missing_knobs.name: mid_channel_exit
missing_knobs.note: exit on a close back below the channel mid

GEN_TRIPLE_EMA_STACK.grid_rationale: smallest grid (8): only touch_atr (pullback proximity to ema_13) and stop_lookback are swept.

fixed_knobs.name: ema_periods
fixed_knobs.reason: the three EMAs are HARDCODED — the defining stack is not swept at all

missing_knobs.name: ema_periods
missing_knobs.note: promote the fixed 5/13/50 stack to swept knobs — currently the strategy's core is untuned

GEN_STOCH_OVERSOLD_CROSS.grid_rationale: stoch_n is the %K window; oversold is the dip threshold; smooth_d is the %D smoothing that %K must cross up through.

missing_knobs.name: overbought_exit
missing_knobs.note: exit when %K crosses down from an overbought level

QL_2026-05-01_SWING_1H_DUAL_RSI_60_40_PU.grid_rationale: rsi_len sets the intraday RSI; daily_hi/daily_lo are the higher-timeframe RSI regime bands (60/40 classic); stop_lookback is the swing-low stop.

fixed_knobs.name: daily_rsi_dependency
fixed_knobs.reason: needs a daily-timeframe RSI feed; returns no signal if absent

QL_2026-05-01_ANY_1H_RSI_CONFLUENCE_PLAY.grid_rationale: rsi_len / sma_len / cross_lvl: entry when price is above the SMA and RSI crosses up through cross_lvl (45/50/55).

QL_2026-05-01_US_EQUITIES_10M_8EMA_PULLB.grid_rationale: shared 8EMA family grid: pullback_atr (proximity to ema_8), impulse_atr (required prior impulse), slope_window.

fixed_knobs.name: ema_period
fixed_knobs.reason: ema_8 anchor is HARDCODED — the family's namesake period is not swept

missing_knobs.name: ema_period
missing_knobs.note: promote the fixed 8 to a swept knob

mtc_v2_parity.note: A standalone review Pine exists but is NOT confirmed input-for-input identical to the engine grid (pullback_atr/impulse_atr/slope_window) or to the global execution model (2R/96-bar/next-open/8bps). A real param->input map requires manual reconciliation against this file before any parity claim; the 3 sibling 8EMA-family strategies share this signal block.

QL_2026-05-01_US_EQUITIES_INTRADAY_LE_MO.grid_rationale: shares the 8EMA family grid and signal block (pullback_atr / impulse_atr / slope_window).

fixed_knobs.name: logic_identical_to
fixed_knobs.reason: same signal block; label differs but rules are identical — provenance/labeling concern

QL_2026-05-01_US_EQUITIES_INTRADAY_PURPL.grid_rationale: shares the 8EMA family grid and signal block.

fixed_knobs.name: logic_identical_to
fixed_knobs.reason: same signal block — distinct label, identical rules

QL_2026-05-01_US_EQUITIES_INTRADAY_8EMA_.grid_rationale: shares the 8EMA family grid; the ONLY strategy using a trailing exit (close below ema_8) instead of the global 2R target.

fixed_knobs.name: exit_style
fixed_knobs.reason: is_trail path in simulate_slice — exits on close<ema_8 next open; does NOT use the 2R target

QL_2026-05-01_UNKNOWN_MULTI_EMA_CHANNEL_.grid_rationale: touch_atr (pullback proximity to long EMA), short_ema/long_ema pair (short<long enforced).

QL_2026-05-01_LIQUID_INTRADAY_VWAP_PULLB.grid_rationale: session_window (rolling-mean VWAP proxy window), prox_atr (proximity to the proxy), slope_window.

fixed_knobs.name: vwap_proxy
fixed_knobs.reason: NOT a true volume-weighted VWAP — approximated by an SMA; known proxy limitation

missing_knobs.name: true_vwap
missing_knobs.note: use a real session-anchored volume-weighted VWAP

QL_2026-05-01_SP500_5M_TWO_CANDLE_SENTIM.grid_rationale: level_lookback (resistance window), upper_third (close position in the bar range), break_buf_atr (breakout buffer).

mtc_v2_parity.note: A standalone review Pine exists (ADA/1h) but is NOT confirmed input-for-input identical to the engine grid (level_lookback/upper_third/break_buf_atr) or to the global execution model. A real param->input map requires manual reconciliation before any parity claim.

QL_2026-05-01_ANY_BOLLINGER_BANDS_20_2_T.grid_rationale: width_quantile (squeeze percentile over a 200-bar window), body_atr (breakout candle body size), bb_len (band period).

fixed_knobs.name: bb_mult
fixed_knobs.reason: upper band = mid + 2*std HARDCODED (the '20_2' name); band width multiplier not swept

missing_knobs.name: bb_mult
missing_knobs.note: promote the fixed 2.0 std multiplier to a swept knob

QL_2026-05-01_ANY_CANDLESTICK_7_PATTERN_.grid_rationale: level_lookback (support window), tolerance_atr (proximity of the pattern to support), atr_stop_mult (ATR-based stop distance — the one swept stop in the library).

fixed_knobs.name: pattern
fixed_knobs.reason: only one of the '7 patterns' (engulfing) is implemented; the named 7-pattern set is not

fixed_knobs.name: atr_period
fixed_knobs.reason: tolerance and stop normalized by df.atr_14

missing_knobs.name: additional_patterns
missing_knobs.note: implement the remaining 6 candlestick patterns the name promises
